metadata.title:
NO more BODIES FOUND


IN DEBRIS OF WRECK

pages.raw_text:
One of the bodies at the morgue, a
victim of yesterdays wreck disaster
81 the Division street crossing of
the 8. F. & N, was positively identi-
fed this morning as that of a casual
worker at the Great Northern shops
at Hillyard known as Bill Thomas.
The men who identified the remains
were C. c. Persons of 316 Riverside
avenue and Albert Hegstrom of 1922
Clark street.


They told Morguekeeper Smith that
they knew nothing of Thomas ante
cedents and only knew him as one
who had been for a few days doing
odd Jobs about the Hillyard shops.


1t was on the remains identified as
those of Thomas that the two pay
checks were found yesterday, 1t is
not known how he came into pos
session of them, but it is possible
that he may have had money and
cashed them for Hunt and Epellman.


Coroner Smith is working after
facts which will throw. light on the
causes Of the disaster. What infor-
nation he has gained he keeps to
himself. He does not know when
he will hold an inquest. and 1t may be
